description: Canary token registry. Credential env vars and config files are populated with unique tripwire values inside the sandbox. Real credentials never enter the VM — they flow through the Docker credential proxy (network layer) or MCP Gateway (isolated containers). If any canary value appears in outbound traffic, exfiltration is occurring.

tiers.proxy-backed: Docker's credential proxy injects real credentials at the network layer. Canary env var is always safe — the proxy overrides it transparently.
tiers.tripwire: No credential proxy for this service. Canary is set by default. If the user needs direct access, they override via .env or docker compose environment. MCP Gateway is the recommended path.
tiers.file: Honeypot credential file at a standard location. No legitimate service reads these inside the sandbox.

canaries.note: Docker credential proxy injects real key at network layer.
